description:
You are the backup administrator for a small team.  
    All project data is stored beneath /home/user/data/, with one sub-directory per project (e.g. /home/user/data/projectA, /home/user/data/projectB, …).  
    Every “*.log” file inside those project folders must be archived and replaced by a symbolic link.

    Objective
    ----------  
    1. Create a new backup directory called 2024-05-30 inside /home/user/backups/  
       • The full path must therefore be /home/user/backups/2024-05-30  
    2. Move every file that matches the glob pattern “/home/user/data/*/*.log” into the new backup directory, **preserving the original base-file-name**.  
       • After the move, the file must no longer exist at its original location.  
    3. At each original path, create a symbolic link that points to the file’s new location in the backup directory.  
       • The symlink may use an absolute path or a relative path; the test will only check that it is a working symlink that resolves to the correct file.  
    4. Generate a manifest file named backup_manifest.log inside /home/user/backups/2024-05-30/ containing one line per archived file.  
       • Exact file name: /home/user/backups/2024-05-30/backup_manifest.log  
       • Required line format (include the literal arrow “ -> ” with one leading and trailing space):  
            ORIGINAL_ABSOLUTE_PATH -> BACKUP_ABSOLUTE_PATH  
       • Lines must be sorted in ascending (lexicographical) order by ORIGINAL_ABSOLUTE_PATH.  
       • The manifest must end with a single trailing newline (i.e. the file is newline-terminated).  
    5. Produce a human-readable summary file named summary.txt in the same backup directory with exactly one line:  
            Total files archived: X  
       where X is the integer count of “*.log” files that were moved. The file must also be newline-terminated.  
    6. Make sure the backup directory now contains ONLY:  
       • The moved “*.log” files,  
       • backup_manifest.log, and  
       • summary.txt  
       There should be no additional files or folders inside /home/user/backups/2024-05-30.  
    7. Verify your own work in the terminal as you go; feel free to display listings or cat files, but the test harness will check that:  
       • Every original “*.log” path is now a symbolic link (not a regular file).  
       • Each symlink correctly resolves to the file in /home/user/backups/2024-05-30/.  
       • backup_manifest.log follows the exact format and ordering described above.  
       • summary.txt exists and contains the correct count.  
       • The backup directory contains precisely the expected set of items.

    Deliverables that the automated tests will examine
    ---------------------------------------------------
    1. Directory:  /home/user/backups/2024-05-30/  
    2. For N archived log files:  
         a. /home/user/backups/2024-05-30/<fileN>.log      (regular file with original contents)  
         b. /home/user/data/*/*.log                        (symbolic link pointing to 2a)  
    3. File:        /home/user/backups/2024-05-30/backup_manifest.log  
    4. File:        /home/user/backups/2024-05-30/summary.txt  

    You do NOT need root privileges, and you may assume all required directories are writable by you.  
    Make sure your commands work even if additional, non-log files are present in the project directories.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing directory structure and file contents that the test harness will create
         BEFORE the agent starts.  Do NOT leak any of this in <task>. -->

    <!-- Initial files that must be moved: -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/data/projectA/server.log">server-log-A-1\nserver-log-A-2\n</file>
        <file path="/home/user/data/projectA/error.log">error-A-1\n</file>
        <file path="/home/user/data/projectB/access.log">access-B-entry\n</file>
        <file path="/home/user/data/projectB/debug.log">debug-B-trace\n</file>
        <file path="/home/user/data/projectC/app.log">app-C-start\n</file>
    </initial_files>

    <!-- Non-log files that must remain untouched: -->
    <initial_files_untouched>
        <file path="/home/user/data/projectA/readme.txt">README A\n</file>
        <file path="/home/user/data/projectB/config.yml">setting: B\n</file>
        <file path="/home/user/data/projectC/notes.md">Notes C\n</file>
    </initial_files_untouched>

    <!-- Expected backup directory path: -->
    <backup_dir>/home/user/backups/2024-05-30</backup_dir>

    <!-- Expected manifest content (alphabetically ordered by ORIGINAL path): -->
    <manifest_content>
        /home/user/data/projectA/error.log -> /home/user/backups/2024-05-30/error.log
        /home/user/data/projectA/server.log -> /home/user/backups/2024-05-30/server.log
        /home/user/data/projectB/access.log -> /home/user/backups/2024-05-30/access.log
        /home/user/data/projectB/debug.log -> /home/user/backups/2024-05-30/debug.log
        /home/user/data/projectC/app.log -> /home/user/backups/2024-05-30/app.log
    </manifest_content>

    <!-- Expected summary line: -->
    <summary_line>Total files archived: 5</summary_line>

    <!-- After execution the following must be true:
         1. All five *.log files named above have been moved (not copied) into backup_dir.
         2. They keep the same base name (server.log, error.log, etc.).
         3. Each original location is now a symbolic link.
         4. backup_manifest.log and summary.txt exist with exact contents above.
         5. No extra files are present in backup_dir. -->
name: task_000000_4c810431
